title: URL Encoder / Decoder
metaDescription: Encode and decode URL components instantly in your browser. Properly escape special characters for query parameters and API calls.
shortDescription: Safely encode or decode text for use in URLs and query parameters.

guideSections.heading: encodeURIComponent vs encodeURI: The Difference That Breaks Query Strings
guideSections.body: encodeURI leaves characters like &, =, and ? untouched because it assumes you're encoding a full URL where those characters are structurally meaningful. encodeURIComponent escapes them too, which is exactly what you want when encoding a single value that will be placed inside a query parameter — using the wrong one is a common source of broken links when a value itself contains an ampersand.

guideSections.heading: Why Decoding Twice Can Silently Corrupt Data
guideSections.body: If a value was already decoded once by a framework or proxy before reaching your code, decoding it again can misinterpret a literal percent sign or transform characters incorrectly. Encoding and decoding should happen exactly once at a clearly defined boundary in your code, not defensively at multiple points just in case.

guideSections.heading: Where This Actually Matters: Search Terms and Special Characters
guideSections.body: A search query containing a space, an ampersand, or a non-English character needs encoding before it can safely become part of a URL — a raw space becomes %20, and an ampersand inside a value becomes %26 so it isn't mistaken for a parameter separator. Testing this tool with a string containing spaces and an ampersand makes the practical difference immediately visible.
